title: ChargePoint's Subscription Surge: A Hidden Gem in EV Infrastructure's Next Phase
text: ChargePoint's subscription revenue grew 13% year-over-year to $162.4 million for the full fiscal year 2026, while networked charging systems revenue declined 8% to $216.5 million. More recently, subscription revenue grew 15% year-over-year to $42 million in the third quarter of fiscal 2026. The subscription business has significantly better margins than hardware sales. Subscription revenue carries a 60% GAAP gross margin, far outpacing the company's overall non-GAAP margin of 24%. ChargePoint's revenue strategy focuses on converting hardware purchasers into long-term subscription customers to lift gross margin and provide predictability. While ChargePoint remains unprofitable overall, the company is making progress. The fourth quarter fiscal 2026 GAAP gross margin was 31% and non-GAAP gross margin was 33%, primarily due to subscription revenue growth as a percentage of total revenue and improvement in subscription margins. Subscription revenue now represents about 20%-25% of total revenue and is the strategic focus for margin expansion. ChargePoint is shifting to a software-led, recurring revenue model, targeting approximately 30% recurring mix by 2027.
